data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Feb 11, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5 pm on Feb 11, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Asian Stocks Soar As Strong US Jobs Data Shakes Rate Cut Bets
data.description:
Asian stocks climbed to record levels on Thursday, propelled by gains in the technology sector, while the dollar strengthened against most major currencies except the yen.

South Korea (.KS11) and Japan (.TOPX, .N225) both reached all-time peaks, helping MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares (.MIAP00000PUS) to another record, up 0.7% and lifting year-to-date gains to around 13%.

Japanese shares have surged following Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s decisive election win over the weekend, with markets expecting increased economic stimulus. The yen rallied 2.7% this week, trading at 153.01 per dollar in afternoon trade, supported by hopes that Takaichi will pursue fiscal responsibility without the need to negotiate with opposition parties.

“Yen shorts are collectively reassessing positions, although at this stage the bearish trend in JPY that started in early 2025 looks more like a reversion to the mean than the start of a structural bull market,” said Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone. “That said, traders need to stay open-minded as the macro picture evolves and where the market decides where it ultimately wants to take JPY.”

Investor attention remains on the US, where a stronger-than-expected jobs report for January has dampened expectations of near-term rate cuts. Job growth accelerated unexpectedly while the unemployment rate fell slightly, signalling labour market stability that could prompt the Federal Reserve to keep rates unchanged.

Thomas Mathews, head of markets for Asia-Pacific at Capital Economics, said, “If labour market conditions are tightening at this point, investors may be overestimating the case for further easing, and Treasuries could be in for a bit more pain.”

Market expectations for a 25-basis-point Fed cut in March had been around 20% before the jobs data but dropped to about 5% after the report, according to CME’s FedWatch Tool. Traders still anticipate at least two rate cuts later in the year. The two-year US Treasury yield jumped 5.8 basis points to 3.505%—its largest single-day gain since late October.

The dollar found support from elevated Treasury yields, rebounding against most currencies. Analysts cautioned, however, that uncertainties around Fed independence and policy risks suggest further positive data will be needed to maintain the recovery.

“Improving global growth prospects and the continued outperformance of non‑US equities keep the case for USD weakness,” OCBC strategists noted.

European futures pointed to a strong opening as investors continue to assess corporate earnings across the region. Market attention is now turning to US inflation data due on Friday, which will be the next key test for expectations of interest rate cuts.

Commodities also reflected geopolitical and market sentiment. Oil prices rose amid concerns over escalating tensions between Iran and the US, with Brent crude up 0.4% at $69.70 per barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude rising 0.46% to $64.93 per barrel. In contrast, spot gold fell 0.44% to $5,058.49 after a more than 1% gain in the previous session.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Mixed As Kee Ming Steals The Spotlight With Explosive Debut
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened on Thursday with a mixed tone as the FBMKLCI dipped slightly by 0.05% to 1,755.52 in early trade, while broader market activity showed pockets of strength among high-profile gainers.

The FBM70, which tracks mid-cap stocks, rose 0.21% to 17,594, and the F4GBM index inched up 0.03% to 1,033.92, signalling a cautious start for investors despite selective buying in active counters.

Kee Ming Group Bhd remained the centre of attention on the ACE Market, surging 46 sen or 123% from its IPO price to 84 sen on strong trading volume of 422,280 lots. The company’s initial public offering was oversubscribed 54.16 times, underscoring investor enthusiasm for the industrial products and services player.

Other gainers at the open included HEIM (+1.04 to 24.74), Carlsberg Malaysia (+0.36 to 18.36), and DLADY (+0.22 to 33.22). On the downside, F&N slid 0.14 to 32.16, MPI fell 0.14 to 30.66, and HLFG was down 0.10 to 22.74.

Among the most actively traded counters were TWL (0.025, +0.005), MMAG (0.045), Bornoil (0.010), and Velesto (0.315, +0.005), reflecting broad interest in both newly listed and smaller-cap stocks.

Market participants are closely watching Kee Ming’s debut performance, which has set the tone for early trading sentiment, even as the benchmark index remains subdued. The mixed opening reflects a balancing act between selective buying in high-profile gainers and caution among blue-chip stocks.

As trading progresses, investors are expected to monitor sectoral movements and corporate developments, with particular attention on newly listed companies that have captured strong retail interest.

data.title: Heineken Lead Gains, Nestle Slide
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia saw notable movements in selected stocks on Wednesday, with HEIM (3255) leading the top gainers, closing at RM24.40, up RM0.70.

F&N (3689) rose RM0.58 to RM32.88, while KEEMING (0392) added RM0.49 to end at RM0.87. Other gainers included HLIND (3301), which increased RM0.42 to RM18.62, and PETGAS (6033), up RM0.30 at RM18.54.

On the other hand, major losses were recorded by Nestle (4707), which fell RM0.70 to RM110.60, and UTDPLT (2089), down RM0.58 at RM29.62. HLFG (1082) eased RM0.16 to RM22.68, while LYSAGHT (9199) declined RM0.14 to RM2.25. Maybank (1155) also slipped RM0.12 to RM12.14.

Market observers noted that trading volumes were particularly active in mid-cap stocks such as KEEMING and HEIM, reflecting selective investor interest amid a broadly cautious market environment.

data.title: Bursa Starts Quietly, Blue Chips Mixed While Traders Watch Overseas Markets
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened largely steady, with the benchmark FBMKLCI rising 0.26 points, or 0.01%, to 1,747.80 in early trade as investors monitored mixed regional and global cues.

Other key indices were slightly higher at the opening bell; the FBM70 added 13.12 points, or 0.07%, to 17,522.53, the FBMEMAS gained 3.73 points, or 0.03%, to 12,775.30, the FBMSHA inched up 1.64 points, or 0.01%, to 12,231.91, and the F4GBM rose 0.12 points, or 0.01%, to 1,029.11.

Trading was led by lower-priced counters, with MMAG topping the most active list at 5 sen, up 0.5 sen. Other active stocks included Velesto at 31 sen, Borneo Oil at 1 sen, TWL Holdings at 2.5 sen, and Mtronic at 1 sen.

Among gainers, HL Industries rose 66 sen to RM18.10, F&N added 22 sen to RM33.40, Heineken Malaysia gained 16 sen to RM24.16, BAT Malaysia climbed 14 sen to RM5.61, and KLCCP Stapled Group advanced 14 sen to RM9.44.

On the losing side, Nestlé (Malaysia) fell 40 sen to RM111.60, Boustead Plantations lost 24 sen to RM15.30, Petronas Dagangan declined 24 sen to RM20.04, Hong Leong Bank shed 22 sen to RM24.76, and MPI slipped 16 sen to RM32.04.

Bursa’s cautious opening mirrored mixed performances in other Asian markets, as investors digested US economic signals.

Overnight, Wall Street saw the S&P 500 and Nasdaq close lower, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average recorded another record finish, reflecting investor hesitancy ahead of upcoming economic data.

data.title: Hang Seng Declines 0.2% On Opening As Investors Stay Cautious On Tech
data.description:
Hong Kong equities opened slightly lower on Thursday, with the benchmark Hang Seng Index declining as investors adopted a cautious stance amid lingering uncertainty over global interest rates, China’s economic outlook and geopolitical developments.

The Hang Seng Index fell 55.82 points, or 0.2%, to 27,210.56 at the opening bell, extending recent volatility as market participants assessed fresh economic data and awaited clearer policy direction from major central banks.

The modest decline reflected cautious investor sentiment following mixed global market performances overnight, as traders balanced signs of economic resilience against concerns over slowing global growth and tightening financial conditions.

Technology stocks, which carry significant weight on the Hang Seng Index, were closely watched as investors evaluated earnings outlooks and demand trends. The sector has remained sensitive to shifts in global technology spending, regulatory developments and interest rate expectations.

Property stocks also remained under scrutiny amid ongoing concerns about China’s real estate sector recovery, which continues to weigh on investor confidence despite recent policy measures aimed at stabilising the market.

Investors are closely monitoring signs of improvement in mainland China’s property market, given its importance to broader economic stability and regional financial markets.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Flat Against Greenback Amid Caution Ahead Of US Labour Data
data.description:
The ringgit opened nearly unchanged against the US dollar on Wednesday, as investors adopt a cautious stance ahead of key United States labour market data release.

At 8 am, the local currency opened at 3.9220/9345 against the greenback from Tuesday’s close of 3.9220/9260. 

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US dollar index (DXY) edged up slightly by 0.05 per cent to 96.865 points, despite weaker-than-expected US retail sales data.

“US retail sales were unchanged month-on-month in December 2025 compared with consensus estimates of 0.4 per cent, suggesting weaker consumer sentiment and slower spending momentum.

“As such, traders will be closely watching the Nonfarm Payrolls and unemployment rate, with consensus estimates at 70,000 jobs compared with 50,000 in December, while the unemployment rate is expected to remain unchanged at 4.4 per cent,” he told Bernama.

Meanwhile, he said traders and investors remain positive on Malaysia, as the ringgit closed higher against the greenback yesterday. 

“High-frequency data continue to support the country’s economy, with the Industrial Production Index (IPI) and the Index of Services growing by 5.0 per cent and 6.5 per cent, respectively, in the fourth quarter of 2025, compared with 4.9 per cent and 5.5 per cent in the previous quarter,” he said. 

At the opening, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the British pound to 5.3465/3635 from 5.3610/3664 at Tuesday’s close, and rose versus the euro to 4.6621/6769 from 4.6731/6778 previously. However, it weakened against the Japanese yen to 2.5382/5464 from 2.5264/5292 at Tuesday’s close.

The local note also traded mostly higher against its ASEAN peers. 

It was slightly higher versus the Singapore dollar to 3.0989/1090 from 3.0994/1028 previously and strengthened against the Thai baht to 12.5420/5920 from 12.5866/6051.

The ringgit was flat against the Philippine peso at 6.70/6.72 from 6.70/6.71 previously. It was also flat against the Indonesian rupiah at 233.3/234.1 from 233.3/233.6 at yesterday’s close.

Bernama

data.title: Cautious Trading Keeps Bursa Mixed On Midday, FBM70 Climbs
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the first half of trading today on a mixed session, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) slipping 6.39 points, or 0.36%, to 1,750.00 as at 12.30 pm.

The broader market was similarly varied, with the FBM70 gaining 27.84 points, or 0.16%, to 17,585.13, while the F4GBM eased 2.24 points, or 0.22%, to 1,031.41. The FBMEMAS index declined 27.98 points, or 0.22%, to 12,800.79, and the FBMSHA fell 11.84 points, or 0.10%, to 12,250.59.

In the stock market, Keeming led trading with 64.8 million shares, rising 0.475 to 0.855, followed closely by Zetrix at 0.830. Pharma slipped 0.010 to 0.315, while Borneo Oil fell 0.005 to 0.005. SMRT advanced slightly by 0.005 to 0.165.

Top gainers during the session included HEIM, which rose 0.660 to 24.360, Keeming, Carlsberg (+0.320 to 18.320), F&N (+0.300 to 32.600), and HLIND (+0.220 to 18.420).

Meanwhile, the leading decliners were Nestle (-0.700 to 110.600), UTDPLT (-0.620 to 29.580), HL Bank (-0.120 to 24.680), KLCC (-0.120 to 9.300) and PPB (-0.100 to 11.000).

data.title: Wall Street Hesitates After Payrolls Surprise
data.description:
Wall Street ended largely flat on Wednesday as a stronger-than-expected US jobs report lifted confidence in the economy but dampened expectations for aggressive interest rate cuts, leaving investors reassessing the Federal Reserve’s next move.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 66.74 points, or 0.13%, to 50,121.40. The Nasdaq Composite fell 36.01 points, or 0.16%, to 23,066.47, while the S&P 500 edged down 0.34 points to close at 6,941.47.

Earlier in the session, all three major indexes had traded firmly higher.

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq touched their highest levels in more than a week after January payrolls data showed US job growth far exceeded expectations and the unemployment rate declined to 4.3%.

However, optimism faded as traders trimmed bets on rate cuts.

Although markets still expect at least one 25-basis-point reduction in June, the probability of rates remaining unchanged that month rose to 41% from 24.8%, according to CME Group’s FedWatch tool.

Julia Hermann, global market strategist at New York Life Investments, said investors digested changes to rate cut bets “quite well” because they interpreted the strong jobs report as good news for the economy.

“This is constructive news in that the economy is not in dire need of rate cuts because the jobs market has been showing some new signs of life,” she said.

Julia added, “It comes down to the sweet spot of hiring being strong enough to show us the economy is resilient but not so strong as to derail expectations for future Fed easing.”

Sector performance was mixed. Energy led gains with a 2.6% rise, followed by consumer staples up 1.4%, while financial services and communication services both dropped more than 1%. Chipmakers advanced, lifting the Philadelphia semiconductor index 2.3%, but software stocks slid 2.6%.

Microsoft fell 2.2% and Alphabet declined 2.4%, weighing on the broader market. Robinhood tumbled 8.9% after missing fourth-quarter revenue expectations, while Generac surged 17.9% on strong results.

Investors are now turning their focus to Friday’s Consumer Price Index report for further clues on inflation and the Fed’s policy path.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Lower, FBM KLCI Slides 0.32%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Wednesday as investors remained cautious amid mixed market signals.

The FBM KLCI (FBMKLCI) slipped 5.54 points, or 0.32%, to 1,750.85, while the broader FBM70 rose 34.33 points, or 0.20%, to 17,591.62.

Other key indices also ended marginally lower, with the F4GBM down 1.87 points (-0.18%) at 1,031.78, FBMEMAS easing 23.33 points (-0.18%) to 12,805.44, and FBMSHA largely unchanged at 12,261.64 (-0.01%).

The session saw active trading in smaller-cap and mid-cap stocks, reflecting selective investor interest.

Among the most active stocks, ZETRIX (0138) closed at 0.820, up 0.005 with 83.6 million shares traded, followed by KEEMING (0392) at 0.870, rising 0.490 on 78.9 million shares. TANCO (2429), PHARMA (7081), and VELESTO (5243) were also heavily traded.

Market analysts noted that sentiment remained cautious as investors weighed corporate earnings against broader economic indicators.

data.title: Crypto Market Slips As Bitcoin, Ethereum Extend Losses
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market traded lower on Feb 11, with Bitcoin and Ethereum both retreating amid cautious sentiment and thinning liquidity.

Bitcoin fell about 2%, slipping below the closely watched US$70,000 level as selling pressure persisted. The world’s largest cryptocurrency traded between an intraday high near US$70,100 and a low just under US$68,000, signalling heightened volatility as bulls struggled to regain momentum.

Ethereum underperformed Bitcoin, sliding more than 4% and hovering around the US$2,000 mark. The weaker showing dragged the Ethereum/Bitcoin ratio lower, reflecting continued investor preference for Bitcoin over major altcoins amid risk aversion.

Overall, market sentiment remained cautious, with traders watching key support levels for signs of stabilisation.

While some market participants see early signs of a potential base forming, analysts warn that further downside risks remain if current support zones fail to hold.

data.title: HL Industries Leads Gainers While MPI Tops Losers
data.description:
HL Industries Bhd led the list of top gainers on Bursa Malaysia at Wednesday’s close, rising 76 sen to RM18.20, albeit on modest volume of 12,440 shares.

British American Tobacco (Malaysia) Bhd climbed 68 sen to RM6.15 with 325,910 shares traded, while Malayan Banking Bhd added 26 sen to RM12.26 after 2.24 million shares changed hands.

KSL Holdings Bhd rose 23 sen to RM3.28 and LPI Capital Bhd gained 20 sen to RM15.38, rounding out the day’s top five gainers.

On the losing side, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd was the biggest decliner, falling RM1.40 to RM30.80 with 20,780 shares traded.

Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd dropped 88 sen to RM32.30, while Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd slipped 70 sen to RM111.30. Heineken Malaysia Bhd fell 30 sen to RM23.70 and BLD Plantation Bhd eased 24 sen to RM15.30.

Profit-taking in selected consumer and industrial counters offset gains in financial and selected mid-cap stocks, leaving the broader market mixed despite the benchmark index closing higher.

data.title: Gold, Silver Gain As Falling US Yields Signal Softer Economy
data.description:
Gold and silver prices climbed on Feb 11 after US Treasury yields retreated, following data showing US retail sales stalled in December, an early sign of cooling economic momentum ahead of key labour and inflation reports.

Reuters reported that spot gold rose 0.3% to US$5,038.73 an ounce, while April gold futures gained 0.6% to US$5,060.60. Silver rebounded 1% to US$81.49, recovering from a sharp sell-off in the previous session.

The move was driven by a drop in US bond yields after a string of weaker data suggested the economy may be losing steam, increasing expectations that the Federal Reserve could cut interest rates later in the year.

Markets are pricing in at least two 25-basis-point rate cuts in 2026, with the first expected in June. Attention now turns to the January non-farm payrolls report due later on Feb 11, followed by inflation data on Feb 13.

Elsewhere, platinum rose 0.6% to US$2,098.78, while palladium edged 0.2% higher to US$1,712.25.

data.title: Asian Stock Markets Mixed As Traders Weigh US Data, Await Jobs
data.description:
Asian stock markets fluctuated on Wednesday after a strong start to the week as investors weighed weak US consumer data that boosted the case for more interest rate cuts but stoked concerns about the world’s number one economy.

The tepid performance followed a mixed day on Wall Street, where tech firms pared recent gains amid lingering worries about extended valuations and the vast sums pumped into artificial intelligence.

Traders were also awaiting key jobs data due later in the day — and inflation figures at the end of the week — that could provide a clearer idea about the Federal Reserve’s plans ahead of its March policy meeting.

The Commerce Department said Tuesday that US retail sales saw no growth in December, having grown 0.6 per cent in November.

The figures provided the Fed with room to consider cutting borrowing costs next month, having held in January after three successive reductions.

However, they also indicated there was unease among American consumers, who are a key driver of growth, and pointed to further weakness in the economy.

And analysts said the previous “bad news is good news” adage, which has fuelled rate cut hopes in the past and propelled markets higher, might be a thing of the past.

“The market is no longer responding uniformly to the idea that weaker data automatically lifts stocks,” said SPI Asset Management’s Stephen Innes.

“Beneath the surface, anxiety around AI-related headline risk is clearly elevated.”

Traders now see a greater chance of three rate cuts this year, with two already baked into prices, according to Bloomberg.

Still, two decision-makers at the central bank made the case Tuesday to keep borrowing costs unchanged for now owing to elevated inflation.

Cleveland Fed boss Beth Hammack said in a speech in Ohio that “based on my forecast, we could be on hold for quite some time”, adding that “I’d prefer to err on the side of patience as we assess the impact of recent rate reductions and monitor how the economy performs”.

And Dallas Fed chief Lorie Logan told a forum in Texas another cut could be appropriate if there were “further material cooling in the labour market”, but she was currently “more worried about inflation remaining stubbornly high”.

While the Dow edged up to another record on Wall Street, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq dropped, with tech firms among the main losers.

Asia fared marginally better, with Hong Kong, Sydney, Seoul, Taipei and Manila up, while Shanghai, Singapore and Wellington were down.

Tokyo was closed for a holiday.

There was little major reaction to data showing Chinese consumer inflation eased last month.

Traders remain on guard about developments in the tech space as they worry that the hundreds of billions of dollars firms have pumped into AI may not see any returns for some time.

That was compounded on Tuesday after Google parent Alphabet raised more than $30 billion in debt in less than 24 hours as it looks to ramp up its capabilities.

News that startup Altruist Corp had rolled out a tax-strategy tool added to the sense of unease on trading floors as it fanned concerns that the software would take business from mainstream firms.

Sentiment was rattled last week after Anthropic unveiled a model that could replace numerous software tools, including those for legal work and data marketing.

data.title: Bullish Territory Remains For Hang Seng Index
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), recommending traders hold long positions as the contract continues to show improving bullish momentum.

The research house said HSIF staged a third consecutive rebound on Wednesday, rising 51 points to close at 27,226 points after opening at 27,189 points. The contract touched an intraday low of 27,138 points and a high of 27,358 points before forming a bullish Doji candlestick. In the evening session, the index eased slightly, last trading at 27,206 points.

RHB Research noted that the relative strength index has hooked higher, signalling strengthening momentum and giving the technical advantage to buyers. With prices holding above the rising 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, the broader technical structure remains firmly bullish, the research house said.

If the upward momentum persists, HSIF may advance to test the 28,600-point resistance level, followed by 29,100 points. On the downside, support is seen at 26,400 points, which also serves as the revised stop-loss threshold, and then at 25,500 points.

RHB Research added that traders should continue to hold the long position initiated at 21,416 points in April 2025, in line with the prevailing uptrend, while monitoring key support levels to manage trading risks.

data.title: Bursa Picks Up Steam With Gains In Heavyweights
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended gains at midday on Wednesday, with investors showing cautious optimism as blue-chip counters led the charge.

The benchmark FBM KLCI added 8.19 points, or 0.47%, to 1,755.73, signalling a steady start to trading.

Broader market indices followed suit, with the FBM 70 rising 39.38 points, or 0.22%, to 17,548.79, while the FBM Emas Index climbed 48.67 points, or 0.38%, to 12,820.24.

Shariah-compliant stocks on the FBMSHA edged up 12.79 points, or 0.10%, to 12,243.06, and the FBM F4GBM index gained 4.18 points, or 0.41%, to 1,033.17.

Among the most active counters, Velesto traded flat at 0.31, Pharma rose 0.01 to 0.315, and Tanco inched up 0.01 to 1.38. ZETRIX added 0.02 to 0.805, while Bornoil remained unchanged at 0.01.

Leading the gainers, HLIND surged 0.72 to 18.16, followed closely by BAT, which jumped 0.67 to 6.14. KLCC added 0.25 to 9.55, Maybank climbed 0.24 to 12.24, and SunCon rose 0.22 to 6.33.

On the flip side, MPI slipped 1.22 to 30.98, BLDPLNT eased 0.64 to 14.90, F&N lost 0.46 to 32.72, Heim dipped 0.32 to 23.68, and Allianz fell 0.30 to 22.76.

Overall, the midday session painted a picture of selective optimism, with investors favouring blue-chip and high-volume stocks, while certain consumer and industrial counters faced selling pressure, keeping the market’s rhythm lively and balanced.

data.title: Stock Picks: Mah Sing And Techstore Bhd
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Mah Sing Bhd and Techstore Bhd as near-term trading ideas, citing improving technical momentum in both counters.

The research house said Mah Sing appears set to extend its rebound after closing firmly above the 21-day simple moving average line, supported by a bullish candlestick formation.

The stock has also climbed past the RM1.15 resistance level, signalling what RHB Research described as a shift in the short-term trend towards the upside.

Should the bullish momentum persist, the research house expects Mah Sing to advance towards RM1.25, followed by RM1.30. However, it cautioned that a pullback below the RM1.10 support level would negate the bullish structure and potentially trigger a corrective phase.

Meanwhile, RHB Research said Techstore may continue its upward trajectory after breaking past resistance with a strong bullish candlestick.

The stock has moved above the RM0.26 breakout point and printed a fresh higher high, indicating robust buying interest.

Following the breakout, the research house expects further follow-through buying that could lift the counter to RM0.29 and subsequently RM0.31.

On the downside, a retreat below the RM0.24 support level would invalidate the bullish setup and may spark renewed selling pressure.

RHB Research’s technical outlook suggests both stocks are drawing trader interest, although it emphasised that key support levels should be closely monitored to manage downside risks.

data.title: Yen Jumps As Election Result Boosts Confidence In Policy Outlook, Dollar Softens Ahead Of Payrolls
data.description:
The Japanese yen surged on Wednesday as investors bet that Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s landslide election victory would strengthen control over fiscal policy, lifting confidence in Japan’s economic direction.

The currency rose nearly 1% against the US dollar to 152.94 in Asian trading, extending gains from the previous session. The euro fell 0.7% to 182.27 yen, while sterling slipped 0.73% to 209.04 yen as the Japanese currency strengthened broadly.

Analysts said the election outcome reduced uncertainty over fiscal direction.

Vishnu Varathan, Head of Macro Research for Asia ex-Japan at Mizuho, noted that a decisive mandate could allow the government to pursue a more coherent fiscal strategy with fewer political compromises.

Strategists at Deutsche Bank also pointed to signs that more market-friendly policies may be emerging, prompting them to close bearish positions on the yen and adopt a neutral stance.

The dollar, meanwhile, remained under pressure ahead of key US non-farm payrolls data due later in the day.

The greenback was weighed down by weaker-than-expected US retail sales and slower labour cost growth, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve may ease policy later in the year.

The dollar index slipped 0.33% to 96.60, while the euro rose to US$1.1914 and sterling edged up to US$1.3658.

Elsewhere, the Australian dollar climbed above US$0.71 for the first time in three years, supported by hawkish signals from the Reserve Bank of Australia.

Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser said inflation remains too high, while OCBC currency strategist Moh Siong Sim raised his year-end forecast for the currency to US$0.73 following the central bank’s recent rate hike to 3.85%.

Investors are now focused on US labour market data, with economists expecting payrolls to have risen by about 70,000 in January, a figure that could shape expectations for the Federal Reserve’s policy path in the months ahead.

Reuters

data.title: STI Opens Firmer Near 5,000 Mark
data.description:
Singapore shares opened slightly higher on Thursday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) inching closer to the psychologically significant 5,000 level as banking stocks and key blue chips provided early support in cautious trading.

As at 9.05 am on Feb 12, the STI rose 14.79 points, or 0.3%, to 4,999.37, placing the benchmark index just shy of the milestone level that traders have been watching in recent sessions.

Market breadth was slightly negative, with 76 advancers against 103 decliners, suggesting that gains were concentrated in selected heavyweights rather than broad-based buying across the board. Total trading volume stood at 78.19 million shares with a value of about S$223.30 million in early trade.

Among actively watched counters, DBS rose 0.748% to S$57.93, helping to anchor the index. Other index constituents also traded in positive territory or held steady, including Singtel at S$4.97, UOB at S$39.07 and OCBC Bank at S$21.68. Keppel changed hands at S$12.50, while Wilmar International traded at S$3.59 and Yangzijiang Shipbuilding stood at S$3.41.

Across the broader market, investors continued to keep an eye on regional and global cues, with derivatives trading showing activity in contracts such as the MSCI Singapore Index Futures and major regional index futures, reflecting ongoing positioning by institutional participants.

Meanwhile, sector indices including the iEdge S-REIT Index and the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index were also monitored by investors for signals on real estate investment trusts and mid-cap performance, which often provide clues on risk appetite beyond the blue-chip segment.

For now, the STI’s proximity to the 5,000 level is likely to remain a focal point for traders, with market participants watching whether sustained buying in banks and large-cap counters can push the benchmark decisively above the threshold in the sessions ahead.
